items.title: Argentina beats Switzerland to set up World Cup 2026 semifinal with England

items.content: Argentina need extra time to beat Switzerland 3-1 in the quarterfinals to set up a showdown with England on Wednesday.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina reach semi-finals after extra-time battle with 10-man Switzerland
items.standfirst: Argentina booked their place in the semi-finals with a dramatic 3-1 extra-time victory over Switzerland in the quarter-finals, with the Nati's numerical disadvantage ultimately proving decisive. Despite Embolo's dismissal reducing Switzerland to ten men, the Alpine underdogs fought valiantly before Argentina's greater experience and depth secured passage to the final four.
items.content: Argentina booked their place in the semi-finals with a dramatic 3-1 extra-time victory over Switzerland in the quarter-finals, with the Nati's numerical disadvantage ultimately proving decisive. Despite Embolo's dismissal reducing Switzerland to ten men, the Alpine underdogs fought valiantly before Argentina's greater experience and depth secured passage to the final four.

items.title: Messi and Argentina survive another close call to reach the World Cup semifinals
items.standfirst: Argentina was taken to the brink in its first two knockout games. In Saturday's quarterfinal against Switzerland, the Albiceleste survived again to advance to the semis, where they will face England.
items.content: Argentina was taken to the brink in its first two knockout games. In Saturday's quarterfinal against Switzerland, the Albiceleste survived again to advance to the semis, where they will face England.(Image credit: Odd Andersen)

items.title: World Cup 2026: Bellingham's brace sends England past Norway to semi-finals
items.standfirst: England booked their place in the semi-finals with a 2-1 extra-time victory over Norway in the quarter-finals, with Jude Bellingham's brace proving decisive. The Three Lions came from behind to overcome a resilient Norwegian side led by Erling Haaland, progressing after an intense extra-time battle in Miami. They will take on the winner of the Argentina-Switzerland match.
items.content: England booked their place in the semi-finals with a 2-1 extra-time victory over Norway in the quarter-finals, with Jude Bellingham's brace proving decisive. The Three Lions came from behind to overcome a resilient Norwegian side led by Erling Haaland, progressing after an intense extra-time battle in Miami. They will take on the winner of the Argentina-Switzerland match.

items.title: In a nailbiter, England moves on to the World Cup semifinals, defeating Norway 2-1

items.content: England's Jude Bellingham has done it again. Scoring both of his team's goals in a thrilling quarterfinal against Norway that needed extra time. It was the first World Cup meeting between the two.(Image credit: Richard Pelham)

items.title: Ann Widdecombe: police believe former MP was attacked almost 24 hours before being found dead – as it happened
items.standfirst: Devon and Cornwall force now looking for suspect ‘believed to be a white male’ after man arrested on Friday was released without chargeAlison and Simon Gilbert, who have lived in in Haytor Vale for over a decade, said Ann Widdecombe was a well-known figure locally. Ms Gilbert told PA media:She was a nice woman, really nice woman, and she had a great sense of humour. It’s a lovely area – you talk to strangers.Everyone saw her as quite an opinionated politician, but to us she was just a person in the community.I never met (Ann Widdecombe) although I’ve lived here all my life.It’s tragic, someone gives all their life to public service and then they end like that. Continue reading...
items.content: Devon and Cornwall force now looking for suspect ‘believed to be a white male’ after man arrested on Friday was released without chargeAlison and Simon Gilbert, who have lived in in Haytor Vale for over a decade, said Ann Widdecombe was a well-known figure locally. Ms Gilbert told PA media:She was a nice woman, really nice woman, and she had a great sense of humour. It’s a lovely area – you talk to strangers.Everyone saw her as quite an opinionated politician, but to us she was just a person in the community.I never met (Ann Widdecombe) although I’ve lived here all my life.It’s tragic, someone gives all their life to public service and then they end like that. Continue reading...

items.title: Typhoon slams into eastern China after forcing nearly two million people to flee
items.standfirst: Typhoon Bavi weakened to a severe tropical storm on Sunday after making landfall in eastern China, where authorities evacuated nearly two million people and suspended transport as the powerful storm threatened flooding and destructive winds.
items.content: Typhoon Bavi weakened to a severe tropical storm on Sunday after making landfall in eastern China, where authorities evacuated nearly two million people and suspended transport as the powerful storm threatened flooding and destructive winds.

items.title: More than 1 million evacuated as Typhoon Bavi makes landfall in China
items.standfirst: Chinese authorities evacuated more than 1.7 million people as of Saturday as Typhoon Bavi made landfall in the country's eastern province of Zhejiang. Hundreds of flights have also been cancelled, and some high-speed railway services have been halted. Bavi is the second typhoon to impact China in just over a week.
items.content: Chinese authorities evacuated more than 1.7 million people as of Saturday as Typhoon Bavi made landfall in the country's eastern province of Zhejiang. Hundreds of flights have also been cancelled, and some high-speed railway services have been halted. Bavi is the second typhoon to impact China in just over a week.

clusters.title: South Africa 2026 World Cup midfielder Jayden Adams dies at 25
clusters.neutral_headline: South Africa World Cup midfielder Adams dies aged 25
bullet_summary: Police opened an investigation after Adams' body was found in a suburb in central Cape Town
Reported by France24, BBC World

items.title: South Africa 2026 World Cup midfielder Jayden Adams dies at 25
items.standfirst: South Africa midfielder Jayden Adams has died at the age of 25, according to the country's sports ministry on Saturday. Officials have not disclosed a cause of death for the football player who represented his country in the ongoing World Cup. Police opened an investigation after Adams' body was found in a suburb in central Cape Town.
items.content: South Africa midfielder Jayden Adams has died at the age of 25, according to the country's sports ministry on Saturday. Officials have not disclosed a cause of death for the football player who represented his country in the ongoing World Cup. Police opened an investigation after Adams' body was found in a suburb in central Cape Town.

items.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Adams dies aged 25
items.standfirst: South Africa and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25, just weeks after featuring for his country at the World Cup.
items.content: South Africa and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25, just weeks after featuring for his country at the World Cup.

clusters.title: New York City bans deceptive subscription practices
clusters.neutral_headline: New York City bans deceptive subscription practices
bullet_summary: Reported by Reuters Top News, Guardian Business

items.title: New York City bans deceptive subscription practices
items.standfirst: New York City has adopted a rule aimed at stopping subscription traps and other deceptive recurring charges, with enforcement set to begin Oct. 1. The city is also pursuing a separate junk-fee rule that would require sellers to show the full price, including mandatory charges, up front. [theguardian.com]
items.content: New York City has adopted a rule aimed at stopping subscription traps and other deceptive recurring charges, with enforcement set to begin Oct. 1. The city is also pursuing a separate junk-fee rule that would require sellers to show the full price, including mandatory charges, up front. [theguardian.com]

items.title: New York City moves to adopt ban of deceptive subscription practices
items.standfirst: Mamdani administration seeks to ban companies from trapping customers into paying recurring charges and ‘junk fees’New York City has adopted a rule that bans companies from using deceptive subscriptions to trap customers into paying for gym memberships, streaming services and other recurring charges, the city’s consumer protection office said.The rule, which will start on 1 October, promises hefty fines and aggressive enforcement for violators. Companies that do not provide a simple way to cancel could pay $525 per user subscription, back fees and additional fines.The headline on this story was amended on 11 July 2026. It had indicated New York City had instituted a ban on deceptive business practices, whereas it has adopted a rule that must first go through public comment and a hearing. Continue reading...
items.content: Mamdani administration seeks to ban companies from trapping customers into paying recurring charges and ‘junk fees’New York City has adopted a rule that bans companies from using deceptive subscriptions to trap customers into paying for gym memberships, streaming services and other recurring charges, the city’s consumer protection office said.The rule, which will start on 1 October, promises hefty fines and aggressive enforcement for violators. Companies that do not provide a simple way to cancel could pay $525 per user subscription, back fees and additional fines.The headline on this story was amended on 11 July 2026. It had indicated New York City had instituted a ban on deceptive business practices, whereas it has adopted a rule that must first go through public comment and a hearing. Continue reading...
